title: Aglio, Olio Tortellini Soup
summary: A simple twist on the italian classic pasta dish. This one-pot tortellini soup is pure comfort and done in 15.

method.description: Add the garlic and olive oil to a medium saucepan. Set over a medium heat and let the garlic sizzle gently for 2–3 mins, stirring occasionally to stop it catching.
Stir in half of the chilli flakes, then pour in the stock and drop in the frozen spinach. Whack the heat up to high, pop a lid on and bring to the boil. Zest your lemon and set aside.
Once boiling, remove the lid and add the tortellini. Cook following pack instructions, then take off the heat. Taste the broth and season with salt and a good squeeze of lemon juice.
Ladle the soup into bowls. Sprinkle over the remaining chilli flakes if you want more of a kick, then top with some lemon zest to taste and grated Parmesan.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
